req.title: Open checking account
req.statement: A verified customer shall be able to open a checking account with an initial deposit between $25 and $50,000; deposits outside this range shall be rejected.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines a deposit range [$25, $50,000] with hard rejection outside that range and a 403 for unverified customers. I apply EP to cover valid/invalid deposit partitions, BVA to probe exact boundaries (24, 25, 50000, 50001), NEG for the unverified-customer path, DT to cross-product verification state with deposit validity, and STC to confirm the account state transition from non-existent to active with a welcome notification dispatched. Six tests are generated: (1) EP positive mid-range, (2) BVA lower boundary on-point, (3) BVA upper boundary on-point, (4) BVA lower boundary off-point (below), (5) BVA upper boundary off-point (above), (6) NEG/DT unverified customer. Each test is idempotent via a unique customer fixture created and torn down per test.

tests.name: test_open_checking_account_valid_mid_range_deposit
expected: Welcome notification dispatched (notification_sent=true or event queued)
trace: R-FIN-001: verified customer, deposit in [$25,$50000] -> account created
tests.executable:
def test_open_checking_account_valid_mid_range_deposit(api_client, create_verified_customer, assert_notification_sent):
    customer = create_verified_customer()
    payload = {"customer_id": customer["id"], "initial_deposit": 1000.00}

    resp = api_client.post("/accounts/checking", json=payload)

    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "account_id" in body
    assert body["status"] == "active"
    assert_notification_sent(customer["id"], notification_type="welcome")

tests.name: test_open_checking_account_below_minimum_deposit_rejected
expected: Response body contains error code or message referencing minimum deposit
trace: R-FIN-001: deposit < $25 shall be rejected with 422
tests.executable:
def test_open_checking_account_below_minimum_deposit_rejected(api_client, create_verified_customer):
    customer = create_verified_customer()
    payload = {"customer_id": customer["id"], "initial_deposit": 24.99}

    resp = api_client.post("/accounts/checking", json=payload)

    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "detail" in body

tests.name: test_open_checking_account_above_maximum_deposit_rejected
expected: Response body contains error code or message referencing maximum deposit
trace: R-FIN-001: deposit > $50,000 shall be rejected with 422
tests.executable:
def test_open_checking_account_above_maximum_deposit_rejected(api_client, create_verified_customer):
    customer = create_verified_customer()
    payload = {"customer_id": customer["id"], "initial_deposit": 50000.01}

    resp = api_client.post("/accounts/checking", json=payload)

    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "detail" in body

tests.name: test_open_checking_account_unverified_customer_forbidden
preconditions: initial_deposit = 1000.00 (valid deposit to isolate verification failure)
trace: R-FIN-001 precondition: customer.verified=true must hold for account creation

tests.name: test_open_checking_account_state_transition_to_active
preconditions: customer has no existing checking account (state: pre-account)
actions: Create a verified customer with no existing accounts via <create_verified_customer>
Assert no checking account exists for customer via GET /accounts/checking?customer_id=<id>
Assert account now exists and is active via GET /accounts/checking?customer_id=<id>
expected: Pre-state: no checking account for customer (404 or empty list)
Post-state: account exists with status='active' and balance=500.00
State transition: none -> active completed successfully
trace: R-FIN-001: expected outcome 'account created' implies state transition from no-account to active
tests.executable:
def test_open_checking_account_state_transition_to_active(api_client, create_verified_customer):
    customer = create_verified_customer()
    cid = customer["id"]

    pre = api_client.get("/accounts/checking", params={"customer_id": cid})
    assert pre.status_code in (404, 200)
    if pre.status_code == 200:
        assert pre.json().get("accounts", []) == []

    resp = api_client.post("/accounts/checking", json={"customer_id": cid, "initial_deposit": 500.00})
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    account_id = resp.json()["account_id"]

    post = api_client.get(f"/accounts/checking/{account_id}")
    assert post.status_code == 200
    body = post.json()
    assert body["status"] == "active"
    assert float(body["balance"]) == 500.00

tests.name: test_open_checking_account_decision_table_unverified_invalid_deposit
preconditions: initial_deposit = 10.00 (below minimum AND unverified - dual-failure condition)
expected: HTTP 403 Forbidden (verification check takes precedence over deposit validation)
trace: R-FIN-001: DT row [verified=false, deposit=invalid] -> 403 precedes 422 per guard ordering
R-FIN-001: error pathway 403 when customer not verified
